title: Bump Compat API version to supported v1.44
body:
All API versions before version 1.44 are now deprecated, starting with Docker client version 1.29 giving an error:

"API version 1.41 is not supported by this client"

Previously it was backward-compatible for more than 10 years, with version 1.24 being the version in classic Docker 1.12.

It seems like API code changes were already added?

Fixes #27890

The API version had not been updated since 2022:

Issue #14219

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
The Compat API now supports the v1.44 API.
```

comments.body:
One failure from [v1.42](https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version-history/#v142-api-changes):

GET /containers/{id}/attach, GET /exec/{id}/start, GET /containers/{id}/logs GET /services/{id}/logs and GET /tasks/{id}/logs now set Content-Type header to application/vnd.docker.multiplexed-stream when a multiplexed stdout/stderr stream is sent to client, application/vnd.docker.raw-stream otherwise.

There is code, but tests fail.

```
Failed tests (2):
 - 769 [20-containers] vnd.docker.raw-stream docker v1.40
 - 777 [20-containers] hijacked connection header: Content-type: application/vnd.docker.raw-stream
```

Looks like a bug in the test.

It "assumed" old docker (1.41).
LGTM, @mheon @inknos @Luap99 are we ok with the bump for 6.0?

It's a bit of a lie, but I think enough of the changes made it in that it's OK to claim support?
LGTM for me...

@baude since we know that having 1-1 compatibility is virtually possible but practically not, I would say I am ok with this

LGTM too

> It's a bit of a lie, but I think enough of the changes made it in that it's OK to claim support?

I meant that 1.44 is supported by Docker, whileas 1.41 is deprecated and thus not supported :-)

It WORKSFORME, but I think it could use some more tests. Interestingly, it already had some code for 1.42-1.45?
Even though Podman only advertised support for 1.41... Should probably investigate going to 1.47, maybe 1.52

* https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/#api-version-matrix

For now there is Moby v25 EOL as a resting point, but otherwise 1.44 probably needs backporting to Podman 5.0
It seems that Docker v30 and beyond will be more aggressive on dropping API support, for older server versions

* https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27890#issuecomment-3763606824

> Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)

This is considered illegal in other projects, like Kubernetes, so didn't do it.

>  All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)

It failed with some darwin-related error. Probably not related to the change?

```
Failed to build: 
 - Darwin 
+ exit 1
make: *** [Makefile:346: .validatepr] Error 1
```

EDIT: Apparently if fails on x86_64, since it only sets GOOS (but not GOARCH)

* https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27925
@mheon I wonder will this be back-ported also to rhel versions?

podman 5.8 will go into the next RHEL minor, I would not recommend backporting this further.

Anyhow RHEL decisions should not be discussed upstream, if someone wants a backport it must be requested via the RHEL process as we have no direct influence upstream about that. No Jira ticket, no backport.

I would disagree with backports in general - we have at least some of the 1.44 API features in 5.8; we have none of them in 5.7 and earlier, so we'd be claiming a level of compatibility we cannot guarantee.
